    "R1.3": "The formal-guarantee claim is never operationalized experimentally; the evaluation reports task-failure rate and Likert scores but no Lyapunov-related metric (e.g., convergence bounds, region-of-attraction size). rationale.R1.1: The section advances three interlocked claims — gap (ambiguity treated as downstream), contribution (coupling grounding to stability), and novelty (certifiable region of attraction). Any meaningful review must reconstruct whether those claims are mutually coherent and well-scoped.
rationale.R1.2: A latent consistency gap exists between the title claim of 'formal Lyapunov stability guarantees' and the described mechanism (confidence-thresholded switching with no switching-stability analysis). The conclusion also silently drops the Lyapunov framing, reverting to purely empirical language. Checking this chain is central to reviewing the section.
rationale.R1.3: The formal-guarantee claim is never operationalized experimentally; the evaluation reports task-failure rate and Likert scores but no Lyapunov-related metric (e.g., convergence bounds, region-of-attraction size). The transferability claim ('same methodology transfers to other platforms') is asserted without supporting data. Evidence-claim alignment is therefore load-bearing.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical soundness — specifically the logical gap between the Lyapunov stability claim and the confidence-threshold switching mechanism — or is it primarily an editorial/writing-quality pass?
